reviews.review_text:
Okay, I suppose. Easy to learn & master, and enjoyable to play. 

The choppy, awkward escape mode leaves much to be desired, though...

Paradox abandomed this game leaving it with a tons of bugs, troubleshooting takes more time than actual playing
Not even the multiplayer servers work correctly

very good game in my opinion.
can run with low end laptops like the vivobook with intergrated graphics.

No.
1. It's from Paradox
2. They have updated their TOS to point out, in not so clear terms, you do not own your game.
3. If you attempt to play on a Mac, specifically Sonoma, you'll get an error message saying the file is damaged and should be thrown in the bin because the download date was not present. 

So no. Stay away. Buy Rimworld instead. 
Support Stop Killing Games
Fun game to play recommend buying gangs dlc for an extra chellange and psych ward: warens edition. dlc for an easy and efortless cash
I enjoyed the sandbox portion of the game, the freedom of building it how you desire.

You can sink tons of hours into this game. Be as creative as you'd like when creating your prisons.

One negative is the fact bugs still exist in a game thats several years old such as audio bugs, graphics bugs, etc. While exploring escape mode my game somehow started filling the entire prison with an absurd amount of guards.
garbage - how is this game still £25 after 11 years? Cant even get the UI to work correctly without 1/2 of it cutting off screen.
no longer loads, tried spending some time figuring how to get it to run, useless turds. used to run fine without without the paradox loader crap.
Fantastic game, so many options learning what works for each situation, then designing it the way you want it. so good. I enjoy this time and time again.

I have over 1000 hrs into this game... On Xbox, and with as horrible as the console version is.

I ended up liking the game so much, I bought a computer for the sole reason to play more PA. I love this game

If you are just getting into management games this is a bold, but really good start. Your first prison (or ten) probably won't be good, but once you get the swing of things. It becomes pretty straight forward, just very detailed and repetitive.

I imagine the building process on here is a lot faster, but just in case. If you want a really big prison then you have to be patient and expect thats its going to take AWHILE. Take your time, do not rush.

Its better to pay attention to detail rather than winging it, and having be a thorn in your side on top of it being an eyesore later on. Big projects in your prison while youre housing inmates causes a ridiculous amount of contraband as well as many different things, and depending on the location. Inmates will just flat out run off and escape....The more time and effort you invest in your prison, the better it will look/run.

There are some bugs in this game and the AI can be janky at times, but isnt a complete deal breaker and its usually a fairly easy work around

Prison Architect is definitely worth the buy just dont pay $30 for it, wait till it goes on sale, and as far as the DLC's go they're decent, but a good chunk of the content should've been a update to the game, rather than feature locked behind a $6 or $10 paywall. Again wait till they're on sale

Incredibly fun game really tickles my brain and helps me get through boring stuff. I love the versatility on the prisons you can build (indoor/outdoor/etc) and I may purchase some DLCs. Overall the game is good I like the 2d and its really fun when its chaos in your prison.

This was one of my first steam games, and one of my first steam games that I enjoyed playing and while I haven't touched it in ages, I would reccommend anyone who wants to get into city building but wants small scale to figure out if it is the genre for them should start with this game in my own opinion.

Cool prison building and management sandbox with a little campaign in real time.  Seems to run efficiently. Cool art style, limited to a top down view, but doesn't detract from the game.  Good fun to play.

Great micro management, one of my favorite sim games. It doesn't get boring quickly and its adjustable difficulty on a variety of levels is perfect. 9.5/10 Wish there was a sequel with more rooms, objects and logistics/deployment options. Runs easy even on my old laptop.
very smooth to run and enjoyable if you dont want your time consumed lol dont get this game
This game is great, i love building massive prisons, and accidently creating my own MMA fighting ring. Still need to try the campaign mode,

can we please please pleaseeeee get an update or even a dlc to escape mode. legit the only dopwnfall to this game. if the devs hear this it would really bring hype rather then some space dlc. who wants to build a prision in space?? give the community something they want for a change. it might change some perspectives

I like this game but it really irritates me that it is so glitchy still.  Right in the middle of your building project the workers will just quit and not work.  It's also incredibly difficult to schedule anything.

Good game, but paradox ruined it, and I can not suggest that you spend your money on this. Wait until prison architect 2 releases, which might be ass. We don't know  if it's ass right now because the devs don't say anything and all the public tests are under NDAs.

Brilliant game, love trying to have a peaceful prison with gangs and difficulty turned up. Very satisfying once you have a running prison fairly peaceful.

The workshop also adds a lot of variety with full conversion mods which change the general look and can add new systems in. Very fun and fairly simple to play, but can be hard to get a smoothly running prison which is all part of the fun.

Yes I did attach PC specs, fight me.
Games like this are the best. not even 500mb, but there is an unless amount of thing to do and manage. truly oe of the best world building games I've personally played.

After buying PA from Introversion Software, Paradox introduced several bugs into the game which they did not fix before ending support. Most egregious, they changed core features, unchanged since early alpha, in the final update before abandonment. Only buy this if you intend to rollback to the 2018 version.

unfortunately, although this game is quite fun, it is a buggy mess that I cannot recommend anyone purchase. the fact that it is available for sale even though it will never receive any bug fixes for the many MANY game breaking issues that can pop up is shameful.

frequent crashes, logistical errors, terrible performance once you achieve any work, random prison destroying nonsense (cooks just refusing to make food with no clear fix, prisoners being stuck forever and starving to death due to bugs, prisoners being able to murder staff through closed doors and actual walls), and a clear lack of care from developers considering the sequel to this game is in development hell and may never see the light of day are all reasons why you should keep your money (and this game WANTS THAT MONEY because it will sell you 10 DLCs with poorly made game-breaking content just for the paycheck).

buy rimworld and play a game that someone cared about making

Highly Recommend this game, especially when the next game is coming soon.

This game is a really chill experience, if your looking for something to relax and just watch time pass by, this is it. 

The game has a lot of pointless DLC, i have only ever owned the main game, DLC in my opinion is not worth it, there are plenty of good mods that change the game.

The game does get repetitive, but i generally play every now and then, and give a break in between and use different mods to make it feel fresh.

good game, quite moddable, runs well, very fun, and you can do basically whatever tf you want. you can make a nice prison where you treat your inmates well and give them opportunities for rehabilitation or you can create a living hell for everyone unfortunate enough to be brought within your fences.
little buggy sometimes but hopefully pa 2 will be better. in the mean time, its pretty fun if you like management games

IMO Terrible privacy policy and EULA, with unnecessary Paradox loader. Do not install this game. They collect geolocation and hardware IDs,, and just about everything else.
I'd only recommend this game if you enjoy building stuff. Concept of this game was fun, and the devs kept on updating. But I guess this is not my cup of tea.

Buy the game.
Become a warden.
Spend 30 hours on building a decent prison.
Have around a 100 prisoners.
Learn some of the prisoner's behaviour.
Put them in solitary.
Watch them kill guards.
Put them in solitary for 200 hours.
Watch them kill guards.
Put them in solitary for 400 hours.
Watch them kill armed guards.
Make them SuperMax and assign a guard to them.
Torture them by changing SuperMax schedule so that they get half an hour of visiting and an hour of Work/Free Time.
Watch them kill guards.
Put them in solitary for hundreds of hours.
Watch them recruit gang members.
Realise that they were Legendary Prisoners.
Watch them start a riot.
Send in 2 riot police squads.
Send 2 squads of Paramedics.
Send 50 armed guards.
Send 30 Dog Units.
Send 100 Guards.
Station 10 snipers.
Open Freefire.
Watch half of your prisoners die.
Watch as the others including the gang leader survive.
Put them in solitary for the rest of their life.
Get sacked.
Boot up the game.

10/10 would spend 50 hours of my life to torture prisoners and get sacked.
Build Jail, Provide Justice, Random Characters, Fun Time, Much Excite
I let the prisoners fight each other so I can jail them longer and get paid more

After 14 prisoners getting away from my prison, I can safely say that my prison it not up to code by a lot, also the last 3 prisoners died fighting each other super smash bros style. 9/10 will play again

came back after not playing for a while. Without any confirmation it installed a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ DRM on my system. No mention of this, no pop up. Just installed malware on my pc without my permission.
What a ♥♥♥♥♥♥ company

This is one of those games that you can come back to again and again.  For as simple as the graphics are, don't be fooled, you need to keep things going, the prisoners happy, and make money.  One of my top 3 games I can return too and enjoy it.
VERY BAD Game, hard to start give me the abbity for gaurds to kill inmates for no reason, rating -100/10
pretty fun prison sandbox game with lots of cool things to do

The Ultimate Prison Management Simulator That Will Make You Lose Track of Time!
With over 82 hours on record, I can confidently say that Prison Architect is one of the most detailed, challenging, and addictive management simulation games ever made.

Incredibly Deep Management System: Building cells, setting up daily regimes for the inmates, and managing the prison's finances and logistics feel highly complex yet immensely satisfying when everything runs smoothly.

Thrill of Security and Emergency Control: Preventing riots, escape attempts, and contraband smuggling requires real strategy. One wrong move in your layout design, and your entire facility can descend into absolute chaos.

High Replay Value and Varied Modes: Whether you are building from scratch, playing through the Campaign, or trying to break out of your own creation in Escape Mode, this game offers endless hours of entertainment.

A true masterpiece for any fan of tycoon and management strategy games. Highly recommended! 9/10.
A unique innovative twist to builder games and fairly well optimized.
i created a camp where troubled jewish individuals can learn to concentrate better!
I love it.  Just wish I could name my prisoner, all my prisoners - then I would of made DJT as President of his prison cell/block
All the fun of actual prison, without the lengthy trial process!
I'm studying how prisons are actually built just to make the most effiecent prison
great game for colony sim type game lovers.
bought this game to play with a friend, then i enjoyed it so much i just played it alone 10/10
Really fun to play your own prisons after you make them, would recommend.
a great grind very fun would recommend to anyone who likes doing whatever they want with there imagination
